name: NYT Opinion
_format_note: 3–4 slides per carousel. NOT 14. This is a short-form editorial format. Every slide carries essential content — no padding, no rest slides.

color_system._philosophy: Color is environmental, not typographic. Background IS the color. Text is always achromatic (black or white). All chromatic richness on Slide 1 comes from the illustration. All chromatic richness on Slides 2–4 comes from the flat color fill. Colored text never appears.
color_system._source_rule: Signature color is DERIVED from the Slide 1 illustration's dominant hue — not pre-assigned from a keyword routing table. The illustration is chosen first; the color follows.

signature_hue_examples._note: These are observed examples from real NYT Opinion carousels — not a fixed routing table. Use as reference for what colors are appropriate.

forbidden: Colored text (always black or white — never a hue on text)
Two-color backgrounds (always a single flat fill)
color_system.contrast_requirement: Black text (#1A1A1A) on signature color background must achieve minimum 4.5:1 contrast ratio (WCAG AA). If color is too dark, lighten. If dark Slide 1 is required, use light tonal variant for quote slides (Wuthering Heights pattern: dark crimson Slide 1 → warm cream Slides 2–4).

headline.color: white or black depending on Slide 1 illustration background
headline.fallback: Noto Serif Display Bold → Georgia Bold → Times New Roman Bold
headline.personality: Institutional authority — 170 years of confident statement-making. Neither delicate (Didone) nor crude (slab). Sturdy, measured, unshowy.

pull_quote.size_scales_with: word count (30–70 words at 48–50px, 60–80 words at 40–44px)
pull_quote.note: Curly typographic quote marks only — never straight/dumb quotes. Quote text is a monolithic block — no paragraph breaks, no bullet points, no sub-headers within the quote.

byline.fallback: Trade Gothic → Helvetica Bold → Arial Bold
byline.note: Byline is metadata, not content. At 16–20px it is legible only if you look for it. Small size + caps + tracking = visual texture, not prose.

size_ratios._note: This is the defining typographic fact. In most systems, headline dwarfs body. Here, pull quotes are nearly headline-sized — because they ARE the content. Both need poster-scale legibility.

rules: Left-aligned always. Never centered, never justified.
No bold, italic, underline, color, or size-change emphasis within quote text. Emphasis comes from the writing, not the typography.
Only ONE serif family (Lora) and ONE sans family (Libre Franklin) across all slides.
Title Case for headlines only. Sentence case for quotes. ALL CAPS for bylines. These never swap.

illustration_zone.fills: full-bleed horizontal — edge to edge, no side margins
illustration_zone.text_overlap: NEVER — illustration and text zones do not overlap

slide_2_4_quote_cards.background: SIGNATURE COLOR full-bleed — single flat solid fill, no texture

quote_zone.fills: 60–80% of vertical space between top margin and byline
quote_zone.content: pull quote — one unbroken passage, monolithic text block

slide_2_4_quote_cards.negative_space_rule: Minimum 10% canvas height (135px) between quote block's last line and byline. Empty space filled with signature color — passive, generous, breathing room.

narrative_arc._note: 3–4 slides only. Every slide is essential. No rest frames, no padding slides.

slide_1.purpose: Stop the scroll. State the claim. Establish voice and visual world.
slide_1.headline_rule: 4–10 words. Must contain a tension, claim, or provocation.
headline_patterns: Declarative Return Report: 'I Just Returned From X. Y.'
Empathy Gap: 'What We Don't Understand About X'

slide_2.purpose: The most shareable, surprising, or provocative line from the argument.
slide_2.quote_rule: 30–70 words. The line someone would screenshot. Contains the core reframe.

slide_3.purpose: The fact, analogy, or escalation that makes the thesis credible.
slide_3.quote_rule: 40–80 words. A specific number, vivid comparison, or named entity.

slide_4.purpose: Emotional or philosophical payoff. Or call to action.
slide_4.quote_rule: 30–60 words. A contrast pair, CTA, or compassionate observation.

pull_quote_rhetorical_structures.hard_fact: [Devastating statistic or uncomfortable truth.] [Reframe that makes it concrete.] [Simple implication the reader cannot argue with.]
pull_quote_rhetorical_structures.escalation: [The first problem stated.] [How it compounds.] [The systemic consequence that results.]
pull_quote_rhetorical_structures.counterintuitive: [The common assumption.] [The actual reality.] [What this means for the reader.]
pull_quote_rhetorical_structures.solution_declaration: [The new reality stated plainly.] [Why it works.] [What it replaces or makes unnecessary.]
pull_quote_rhetorical_structures.process_as_experience: [What happens first — second person.] [What happens next — instant, simple.] [What the reader feels — or does not have to feel.]
pull_quote_rhetorical_structures.cta_parenthetical: [The stakes of not acting.] [The specific action.] [(You can [specific action] through [brand name].) — parenthetical, not a button]

density_rhythm._note: The carousel front-loads all visual richness into Slide 1 then maintains a steady typographic plateau. Inverse of Dark Museum (which builds and releases). Here, the single visual shift is Slide 1 → Slide 2: split-zone illustrated card to full-bleed typographic card.

illustration_system._philosophy: The Slide 1 illustration is a visual argument, not a visual description. It embodies the article's THESIS — not its topic — as a single, instantly readable image. The metaphor must work in under 2 seconds at phone-scroll speed.
illustration_system._note: Images appear ONLY on Slide 1. Slides 2–4 are image-free.

mode_1_editorial_cartoon.name: Editorial Cartoon
mode_1_editorial_cartoon.characteristics: Bold saturated colors, clean vector rendering, sharp edges, no gradients within shapes. Political cartoon meets mid-century editorial illustration (Ben Shahn, Push Pin Studios).
mode_1_editorial_cartoon.example: Golfer putting through a brick wall = tariffs aren't blocking trade (China trade piece)
mode_1_editorial_cartoon.prompt_template: Editorial illustration, flat graphic style. {scene_embodying_thesis}. Bold saturated colors: {primary_color} and {secondary_color}. Clean vector rendering, sharp edges, no gradients within shapes. Political cartoon meets mid-century editorial illustration. Stylized, not photorealistic. Strong geometric composition. No text, no labels, no border. 4:5 portrait.

mode_2_fine_art.name: Fine Art / Expressive
mode_2_fine_art.used_for: Profiles, mental health, personal essays, human-interest pieces
mode_2_fine_art.characteristics: Heavy texture — paper grain, printmaking plate marks, charcoal dust, watercolor bleeds. Texture = emotional truth. Muted palette.
mode_2_fine_art.example: Overlapping etched faces = fractured self-perception (mental health piece)
mode_2_fine_art.prompt_template: Fine art editorial illustration, {medium: drypoint etching / charcoal monoprint / watercolor wash} style. {subject_description}. {emotion: raw / ghostly / tender / unsettling}. Muted palette: {base_tones} with accents of {accent_color}. Textured, hand-rendered, visible mark-making. Traditional printmaking aesthetic. No text, no border. 4:5 portrait.

mode_3_conceptual_photography.name: Conceptual Photography
mode_3_conceptual_photography.used_for: Literary criticism, cultural essays, gothic or dramatic topics
mode_3_conceptual_photography.characteristics: Dramatic single-source lighting, deep shadows, rich material reality. Objects feel alive and present.
mode_3_conceptual_photography.example: Hand holding glowing snake = dangerous allure of Wuthering Heights
mode_3_conceptual_photography.prompt_template: Dramatic conceptual photograph, studio shot. {subject_description}. {lighting: chiaroscuro / rim-lit / single spotlight}. Color palette: {dominant} to {secondary}. {mood: gothic / sensual / clinical / reverent}. High production editorial photography. Sharp focus, dramatic shadow fall-off. No text, no border. 4:5 portrait.

mode_4_documentary.name: Documentary Photography
mode_4_documentary.used_for: Obituaries, political legacy pieces, historical events
mode_4_documentary.characteristics: Film grain, archival patina, environmental lighting. As-captured. Historical authenticity.
mode_4_documentary.example: Jesse Jackson at the 1988 Democratic Convention (political legacy piece)
mode_4_documentary.prompt_template: N/A — use archival photography source, not AI generation.

metaphor_selection_rubric.step_1: Extract the THESIS, not the topic. 'We are losing the trade war' is the thesis; 'US-China relations' is the topic.
metaphor_selection_rubric.step_2: Find a physical scenario that embodies this tension — a real-world scene, not just an object.
metaphor_selection_rubric.step_3: Layer the metaphor: primary read (obvious), secondary (cultural connotation), tertiary (ironic detail).
metaphor_selection_rubric.step_4: Ensure instant legibility — test: does someone understand the FEELING in 1 second without the headline?
metaphor_selection_rubric.step_5: Check color inheritance — the illustration must have one dominant hue that can sustain 3 flat-color quote slides.

competition_losing.approach: Games with obstacles, races with handicaps, sports against immovable forces

broken_systems.approach: Machines malfunctioning, structures crumbling, tools that don't fit
scenarios: Key that doesn't fit the lock

mental_health_inner_struggle.approach: Fractured or doubled selves, distorted perception, invisible barriers

dangerous_beauty.approach: Creatures beautiful and harmful, flames, thorned flowers

legacy_impact.approach: Ripples, footprints, shadows, crowds shaped by one figure

moral_urgency.approach: Seeds, sprouts, simple generous acts, hands reaching

hypocrisy_gap.approach: Masks, facades, optical illusions, Potemkin structures
scenarios: Painted facade with crumbling wall behind

inequality_injustice.approach: Scales tipped, ladders missing rungs, divided landscapes

anti_patterns: The literal depiction — a chip for semiconductors, a brain for mental health, a book for literary criticism
The cliché — globe for global, handshake for diplomacy, broken chain for freedom
The multi-scene — more than one tension or scenario in one illustration
The chromatically confused — 5+ competing hues with no dominant color to inherit
The too-clever — requires insider knowledge to decode, fails the 2-second legibility test

logo.color: monochrome — white on dark, black on light
logo.function: Colophon, not logo — like a publisher's mark on every page. Always present, never dominant.

brand_integration.brand_color_rule: Brand colors do NOT appear in this system. The per-carousel signature color overrides any brand palette. Brand identity comes from the typographic system, not from color.

voice_alignment.authority: Declarative sentences. No hedging. → Bold serif type. No decorative softening.
voice_alignment.restraint: No exclamation marks. Dry wit over punchlines. → No gradients, icons, embellishments.
voice_alignment.directness: 'We are not winning.' Short, declarative. → Left-aligned. Large type. No compositional ambiguity.
voice_alignment.inclusivity: 'We' and 'our' — implicates the reader. → Full-bleed color wraps around the viewer.

quality_checklist: Does the illustration embody the article's THESIS, not just its TOPIC?
Is the illustration legible as a metaphor in under 2 seconds?
Does the illustration's dominant color support 3 flat-color quote slides?
Is ALL text left-aligned (never centered, never justified)?
Is the headline in Title Case?
Are bylines in ALL CAPS with visible letter-spacing?
Is there only ONE serif family + ONE sans family across all slides?
Is the logo present on every slide, bottom-right, monochrome?
Are quote cards FREE of any imagery, icons, or decorative elements?
Does each quote stand alone (readable without the article)?
Is quote text black on the colored background (never colored text)?
Is there adequate breathing room (10%+ canvas height) between quote block and byline?
Do the quotes follow the Thesis → Evidence → Landing progression?
Does black text on signature color achieve minimum 4.5:1 contrast ratio?
do_not_list: Colored text on quote cards — always black
Multiple fonts — only Lora + Libre Franklin
Two-color backgrounds — single flat fill only
Bullet points or sub-headers within quotes
Screenshots or product UI within illustrations
Text within the illustration (no labels, signs, speech bubbles)
14 slides — this is a 3–4 slide format
